articles.title: Mother dies saving daughter in Venezuela earthquakes
articles.summary: Héctor Bello wrote on social media that "you gave your own life for our daughter" in the quakes which killed at least 920 people.

articles.title: DR Congo takes Rwanda to international court over decades of conflict
articles.summary: Kinshasa accuses its neighbour of committing various violations since the 1994 Rwanda genocide.

articles.title: Trump accuses Iran of ceasefire breach after Strait of Hormuz attack
articles.summary: A Singapore-flagged ship was struck crossing the waterway on Thursday, prompting a large-scale evacuation to be paused.

articles.title: Ex-Trump adviser John Bolton pleads guilty to mishandling classified documents
articles.summary: Bolton faces a prison sentence of up to five years and has agreed to pay $2.25m in fine, prosecutors say.

articles.title: Christmas market attacker jailed for life for murdering six in Germany
articles.summary: A nine-year-old and five women were killed when Taleb Al-Abdulmohsen drove into the market in 2024.

articles.title: Reflecting Pool liner cut with sharp knife or razor, National Park Service says
articles.summary: Earlier in the week Donald Trump blamed vandals for "a 300 foot long gash" in the pool and accused someone of putting fertiliser in the water.

articles.title: Senior Ukrainian intelligence official jailed for life for spying for Russia
articles.summary: Col Dmytro Kozyura agreed to share state secrets and systematically disclosed classified information, prosecutors say.

articles.title: Four men held over child marriage in Sierra Leone appear in landmark court case
articles.summary: It relates to a 17-year-old girl - four men including her father and husband face years in jail if convicted.

articles.title: Canada eligible to join Eurovision competition
articles.summary: Prime Minister Mark Carney raised the idea of Canada joining the song contest in his 2025 budget.

articles.title: US sanctions Rwanda gold refinery accused of smuggling DR Congo's minerals
articles.summary: The US alleges Gasabo Gold Refinery smuggled at least 60kg of gold in early 2026.
